notes.text: Elizabeth Bennet seeks autonomy and clarity in a world pressuring her toward pragmatic marriage.
Elizabeth and Mr. Darcy meet with instant friction, attraction masked by pride and prejudice.
Both resist emotional vulnerability: Elizabeth distrusts Darcy, Darcy judges too quickly.
Social events and family ties repeatedly force them into each other's orbit.
Darcy's actions and Elizabeth's reflections reveal deeper respect and emotional truth.
Fear and misunderstanding trigger retreat before either can commit honestly.
Crisis peaks around Lydia's scandal and wounded trust, threatening any future together.
Darcy's quiet intervention and renewed proposal become the emotional grand gesture.
They choose each other with new humility and mutual respect.
